title: Urban Legends
description: Modern rumors, cautionary tales, social legends, and stories that spread because they feel almost possible.

title: Internet Folklore
description: Digital legends, online myths, and stories born in forums, images, games, shared files, and repeated screenshots.

title: Strange Places
description: Real or legendary places surrounded by unusual records, disappearances, impossible rumors, and uncertain maps.

title: Unexplained Mysteries
description: Careful mystery notes, unresolved questions, and strange records presented without sensational certainty.

title: Classic Folklore
description: Older oral traditions, inherited warnings, local beliefs, folk motifs, and stories carried across generations.

title: Modern Legends
description: Near-modern reported legends, sighting stories, rumor cycles, and cryptid-like accounts shaped by repetition.

title: Myths
description: Traditional mythic stories, creation tales, sacred narratives, gods, heroes, and symbolic explanations of the world.

title: Mythic Creatures
description: Dragons, krakens, giants, spirits, sea beings, forest figures, and creatures shaped by belief.

title: Lost Worlds
description: Lost cities, vanished islands, unmapped lands, impossible geographies, and worlds that resist being fixed.

title: Strange Nature
description: Unusual forests, strange plants, sky and sea phenomena, and natural places that attract impossible stories.

title: Legendary Places
description: Sacred mountains, forbidden lakes, ruins, hidden temples, and named places given power through legend.

title: Mythic Objects
description: Legendary swords, bells, mirrors, relics, books, charms, and objects that seem to carry more than function.

title: Legend Origins
description: Records focused on where a story shape begins, changes, and becomes recognizable.
